source_reports.use_as: terminal ledger baseline only, not visual destination coverage

target_destination_ledger.public_safe_note: TASK-027S repeatedly reached or remained in app shell loader after launcher entry and rail attempts; no session journal destination was visually reached.
TASK-027S repeatedly reached or remained in app shell loader after launcher entry and rail attempts; no Steam/top-up QR destination was visually reached, and no QR target was followed.
TASK-027S repeatedly reached or remained in app shell loader after launcher entry and rail attempts; no feedback QR destination was visually reached, and no QR target was followed.

entry_surface_ledger.public_safe_note: Launcher/recommendations entry is a real entry surface; in TASK-027S it led to a persistent app shell loader, not a loaded catalog or destination.
Explicit launch oracle did not restore the in-app catalog state during TASK-027S.
Alternate package launch oracle still stayed on the Google TV launcher surface.

visual_markers_public_safe: main content spinner or loader visible
xml_markers_public_safe: no clickable nodes exposed for visible rail state
no scrollable nodes exposed for visible rail state
reduced node set compared with normal catalog
state_detection_targets.test_action_after_timeout: record anomaly with screenshot, XML and local-only diagnostics, then recover or move to the next bounded route without waiting indefinitely
hypotheses_public_safe: content bootstrap or shell hydration does not complete after launcher entry
route host remains in a preloader state while the rail shell is already visible
profile/session/entitlement bootstrap or content service response may be unresolved without a user-visible error state
rail focus/action nodes may be registered only after content bootstrap, leaving the visible rail non-interactive

anomaly_records.trigger_action: Activate visible MTC Fog Play app entry from Google TV launcher or recommendation surface.
anomaly_records.expected_result: A loaded post-auth catalog or another recoverable app destination screen appears.
anomaly_records.observed_result: The app shell rail appeared, but the main content area remained on a spinner/loader through delayed captures and a 120-second timeout checkpoint.
anomaly_records.likely_or_hypothesis_cause: likely launcher-entry or shell-hydration/content-bootstrap stall where the navigation shell renders but catalog/content route resolution does not complete; possible profile/session/entitlement wait without user-visible error state
anomaly_records.recovery_action: After up to 120 seconds, classify as app-shell-loader anomaly, collect local-only screenshot/XML/log/dumpsys diagnostics, and move on via safe recovery instead of waiting indefinitely.
anomaly_records.test_design_implication: Automation needs an explicit detector and 120-second timeout for app_shell_loader_after_launcher_entry before rail destination assertions.

anomaly_records.trigger_action: Attempt explicit and leanback launch oracles for the selected app lane.
anomaly_records.expected_result: The in-app catalog rail state appears.
anomaly_records.observed_result: The visual checkpoints remained on a Google TV launcher surface or app recommendation surface.
anomaly_records.likely_or_hypothesis_cause: likely launch-intent or foreground recovery oracle mismatch in the current device state
anomaly_records.recovery_action: Use launcher entry surface as a separate entry path and keep raw package/component details local-only.
anomaly_records.test_design_implication: Entry-surface coverage and in-app destination coverage must be tracked separately.

anomaly_records.trigger_action: Attempt to continue rail navigation after app-shell-loader evidence while device/app had been idle.
anomaly_records.expected_result: The app foreground remains available for the next bounded navigation checkpoint.
anomaly_records.observed_result: A TV/system ambient surface appeared instead of app navigation content.
anomaly_records.likely_or_hypothesis_cause: likely Android TV ambient/screensaver interruption during idle time, not an app destination screen
anomaly_records.recovery_action: Classify as external/system interruption, recover the approved app lane safely, and do not count it as app coverage.
anomaly_records.test_design_implication: Runtime state detector must distinguish app-shell-loader, launcher and external ambient surfaces before making destination assertions.

anomaly_records.trigger_action: From app-shell-loader state, apply bounded D-pad/center attempts and direct taps on visible rail icon regions for journal, Steam/top-up and feedback candidates.
anomaly_records.expected_result: A visually distinct session journal, Steam/top-up QR or feedback QR destination appears, or focus moves to a usable rail target.
anomaly_records.observed_result: The visual checkpoints remained on the app shell loader; XML exposed no focused, clickable or scrollable target nodes for the visible rail state.
anomaly_records.likely_or_hypothesis_cause: likely rail action/focus registration is blocked while content bootstrap is incomplete, or the visible rail is a non-interactive shell layer until content route resolution finishes
anomaly_records.recovery_action: Do not repeat additional coordinate/key attempts from loader without a new oracle; classify destination coverage as not visually reached for this task.
anomaly_records.test_design_implication: Future route tests need a state oracle proving loaded catalog or actionable rail focus before asserting destination transitions.
